steps.title: Identify the antecedent
steps.content: The blank fills "The Eiteljorg Museum counts pieces by S'eiltin in ______ impressive collection." The pronoun must refer to "The Eiteljorg Museum" — singular and an institution (non-person).

steps.title: Rule: use "its" for singular non-person antecedents
steps.content:
Per-choice diagnosis:

A. "its" — singular possessive for non-persons. The Museum and its collection — agreement holds. Correct.

B. "her" — possessive for female persons; The Eiteljorg Museum is an institution, not a person. Incorrect.

C. "it's" — contraction of "it is"; substitution gives "the Museum counts pieces in it is impressive collection," which is ungrammatical. Incorrect.

D. "their" — plural possessive; the antecedent is singular. Incorrect.

A is correct.
